rationale: This is a licensed-IP anime hook (Dokkan Battle/Goku) — visually saturated neon anime art clashes hard with Knock'em All's minimalist pastel 3D look, and we obviously can't use Dragon Ball IP without licensing. The static "PLAY NOW" charging-blast concept also doesn't communicate the tap-to-shoot physics ragdoll loop that drives installs for this game, and it skews to a mid-core anime gacha audience rather than the broad casual hypercasual player. Skip — neither the asset nor the concept is portable; if there's a learning to extract, it's only the very generic "bright character + PLAY NOW CTA" pattern, which we already cover with on-brand creatives.
